title: rand family + optional stream + finite over-approximation
manager: Randomness done so it stays replayable: the rand family takes an optional explicit random stream, so a machine's "random" choice is a pure function of a seed you control — which is exactly what makes seeded walks and deterministic replay possible at all. For the checker, randomness is finitely over-approximated, so a machine that flips coins can still be reasoned about as a finite object rather than becoming unanalyzable the moment it rolls a die.
cs: rand family with an optional stream argument (W6.31); seed-driven and replayable (feeds W6.100); finite over-approximation for the checkable band; a default stream when none is supplied.
